fields.name: Mechanics
fields.description: Motion, forces, momentum, and energy in everyday systems.

fields.name: Quantum Physics
fields.description: Wavefunctions, uncertainty, quantization, and modern devices.

fields.name: Relativity
fields.description: Space, time, gravity, and the structure of the cosmos.

fields.name: Thermodynamics
fields.description: Heat, entropy, engines, and statistical behavior.

fields.name: Optics
fields.description: Light, lenses, interference, and imaging systems.

fields.name: Astrophysics
fields.description: Stars, galaxies, black holes, and cosmic evolution.

fields.title: Newton's Laws Without the Fog
fields.short_description: A clean guide to force, acceleration, and how to reason from free-body diagrams.
fields.full_content:
The central idea

Physics is a compression language for nature. A small model can explain a large range of observations when the variables are chosen carefully.

Start by asking what is conserved, what changes, and what scale matters. Most problems become calmer once those three questions are separated.

F = ma

Build intuition

Imagine tracking a system frame by frame. If motion changes, something transferred momentum. If temperature changes, energy moved. If light bends, spacetime or material structure shaped its path.

given: known quantities
model: choose conservation law
solve: isolate the unknown
check: units and limiting cases

Why it matters

Good physics thinking is not memorizing formulas. It is learning how to see hidden structure in motion, fields, waves, particles, heat, and information.

fields.title: Energy Conservation as a Superpower
fields.short_description: Learn when energy methods make motion problems dramatically simpler.

fields.title: JEE Main: Work done by a constant force
fields.question: A force of 10 N moves a body by 5 m in the direction of force. Find the work done.
fields.solution: Work done by a constant force is W = Fs cos theta. Here theta = 0, so W = 10 x 5 = 50 J.

fields.title: NEET: Lens formula numerical
fields.question: An object is placed 30 cm from a convex lens of focal length 15 cm. The image distance is:
fields.solution: Using 1/f = 1/v - 1/u, with f = 15 cm and u = -30 cm, we get 1/v = 1/15 - 1/30 = 1/30, so v = 30 cm.

fields.title: JEE Advanced: Entropy change idea
fields.question: For a reversible isothermal expansion of an ideal gas, entropy change depends on:
fields.solution: For reversible isothermal expansion, Delta S = nR ln(V2/V1). It depends on the volume ratio.

fields.title: Boards: Escape velocity concept
fields.question: Escape velocity from a planet depends on which quantities?
fields.solution: Escape velocity is v = sqrt(2GM/R), so it depends on planet mass M and radius R.
